title: MongoDB Collection Creation Using Real Student Data Examples
description: Comprehensive guide showing all 15 collection creation methods using actual student data from data.json

methods.title: Implicit Collection Creation - Insert Students
methods.description: Collections are created automatically when you insert the first student document

examples.name: Insert First Student (Create Collection)
examples.query: db.students.insertOne({ studentId: { $numberLong: '20260001' }, name: { first: 'Daksh', last: 'Chauhan' }, age: 20, enrollmentDate: { $date: '2022-08-15T09:00:00Z' }, major: 'Data Science', gpa: { $numberDecimal: '3.85' }, isInternational: false, skills: ['Python', 'SQL', 'TensorFlow'], courses: [{ courseCode: 'DS-401', credits: 4, grade: 'A' }, { courseCode: 'STAT-301', credits: 3, grade: 'A-' }], campusLocation: { type: 'Point', coordinates: [77.2095, 28.6144] }, scholarshipDetails: { scholarshipName: 'Merit Excellence', amount: 15000, renewable: true }, libraryCardToken: { $binary: { base64: 'c3R1ZGVudF9oYXNoXzAwMQ==', subType: '00' } } })
examples.explanation: Creates 'students' collection with first student (Daksh Chauhan)

examples.name: Insert All 60 Students
examples.explanation: Creates collection with all 60 students from data.json
examples.result: Complete students collection with all records

methods.title: Explicit Collection Creation - Students with Options
methods.description: Explicitly create students collection with specific configurations

examples.name: Basic Students Collection
examples.explanation: Creates empty 'students' collection
examples.result: Empty collection ready for student data

examples.name: Students Collection with Size Limit
examples.explanation: Creates collection with 100MB size limit and max 10,000 documents

examples.name: Capped Students Archive
examples.explanation: Creates capped collection for archived students (auto-removes oldest when full)
examples.result: Capped archive collection for legacy student data

methods.title: Collection with Schema Validation - Student Data Integrity
methods.description: Create students collection with strict schema validation

examples.name: Basic Student Schema Validation
examples.query: db.createCollection('students', { validator: { $jsonSchema: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['studentId', 'name', 'age', 'major', 'gpa'], properties: { studentId: { bsonType: 'long' }, name: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['first', 'last'], properties: { first: { bsonType: 'string' }, last: { bsonType: 'string' } } }, age: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 18, maximum: 65 }, major: { bsonType: 'string' }, gpa: { bsonType: 'decimal', minimum: 0, maximum: 4.0 }, isInternational: { bsonType: 'bool' }, skills: { bsonType: 'array', items: { bsonType: 'string' } } } } } })
examples.explanation: Creates students collection with schema validation for all required fields

examples.name: Comprehensive Student Validation with All Fields
examples.query: db.createCollection('students', { validator: { $jsonSchema: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['studentId', 'name', 'age', 'enrollmentDate', 'major', 'gpa', 'isInternational', 'skills', 'courses', 'campusLocation', 'libraryCardToken'], properties: { studentId: { bsonType: 'long' }, name: { bsonType: 'object', properties: { first: { bsonType: 'string', minLength: 1, maxLength: 100 }, last: { bsonType: 'string', minLength: 1, maxLength: 100 } } }, age: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 18, maximum: 65 }, enrollmentDate: { bsonType: 'date' }, major: { enum: ['Data Science', 'Frontend Engineering', 'Machine Learning', 'Cloud Computing', 'Cybersecurity', 'Mobile Development', 'Backend Engineering', 'Artificial Intelligence', 'DevOps Engineering', 'Game Development', 'Full Stack Development', 'Blockchain Development', 'Software Architecture', 'QA Automation', 'Data Engineering', 'UI/UX Design', 'IoT Solutions', 'Technical Writing', 'Database Administration', 'Solution Architecture', 'Network Engineering', 'Product Management', 'Computer Vision', 'Cyber Threat Intelligence', 'Robotics Engineering', 'API Development', 'Site Reliability Engineering', 'Augmented Reality', 'Quantum Computing', 'Bioinformatics', 'Enterprise Software', 'Embedded Systems', 'Web Services Architecture', 'Accessibility Engineering', 'Performance Engineering', 'Natural Language Processing', 'Fintech Development', 'Environmental Computing', 'Healthcare IT', 'E-Commerce Development', 'Marketing Technology', 'Supply Chain Tech', 'Content Delivery Systems', 'Information Security', 'Voice Technology', 'Streaming Platforms', 'Software Testing', 'Container Orchestration', 'Graph Database Design', 'Search Engine Architecture', 'Analytics Engineering', 'Compliance Engineering', 'Edge Computing', 'Software Security', 'API Governance', 'Cloud Security', 'Low-Code Platforms', 'Incident Management', 'Data Privacy', 'Observability Engineering'] }, gpa: { bsonType: 'decimal', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 4.0 }, isInternational: { bsonType: 'bool' }, skills: { bsonType: 'array', minItems: 1, maxItems: 10, items: { bsonType: 'string' } }, courses: { bsonType: 'array', items: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['courseCode', 'credits', 'grade'], properties: { courseCode: { bsonType: 'string' }, credits: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 1, maximum: 4 }, grade: { enum: ['A+', 'A', 'A-', 'B+', 'B', 'B-', 'C+', 'C', 'C-', 'D', 'F'] } } } }, campusLocation: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['type', 'coordinates'], properties: { type: { enum: ['Point'] }, coordinates: { bsonType: 'array', minItems: 2, maxItems: 2, items: { bsonType: 'double' } } } }, scholarshipDetails: { oneOf: [{ bsonType: 'null' }, { bsonType: 'object', properties: { scholarshipName: { bsonType: 'string' }, amount: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 5000 }, renewable: { bsonType: 'bool' } } }] }, libraryCardToken: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['$binary'], properties: { '$binary': { bsonType: 'object', properties: { base64: { bsonType: 'string' }, subType: { bsonType: 'string' } } } } } } } } })
examples.explanation: Creates students collection with comprehensive validation for all 60 student records
examples.result: Fully validated students collection matching data.json structure

methods.title: Collection with Collation - Multi-Language Student Names
methods.description: Create students collection with collation for proper name handling

examples.name: English Collation for Student Names
examples.explanation: Creates students collection with English US collation for proper sorting of student names
examples.result: Students collection with English name sorting

examples.name: Case-Insensitive Student Queries
examples.explanation: Enables case-insensitive queries on student names (Daksh = DAKSH = daksh)

methods.title: Time Series Collection - Student Enrollment Data
methods.description: Create optimized collection for student enrollment time series

examples.name: Enrollment Timeline Collection
examples.explanation: Creates time series collection for tracking student enrollments over time
examples.result: Time series collection for enrollment analytics

examples.name: GPA Tracking Over Time
examples.explanation: Creates monthly granularity time series for student GPA progression

methods.title: Collection with Indexes - Optimize Student Queries
methods.description: Create students collection with optimized indexes

examples.name: Index on Student ID
examples.explanation: Creates index on studentId for fast lookups
examples.result: Indexed studentId field for quick retrieval

examples.name: Index on Major (Group by Major)
examples.explanation: Creates index on major field to quickly find all students by major

examples.name: Index on Age Range Queries
examples.explanation: Creates index for efficient age range queries
examples.result: Age field indexed for range queries

examples.name: Compound Index - Major and GPA
examples.explanation: Creates index for finding top GPA students by major
examples.result: Compound index for major and GPA sorting

examples.name: Text Index on Skills
examples.explanation: Creates text search index for skill-based queries

examples.name: Geospatial Index on Campus Location
examples.explanation: Creates geospatial index for location-based student queries

examples.name: Unique Index on Student Email (if added)
examples.explanation: Ensures no duplicate student emails

methods.title: Collection with TTL - Student Session Cache
methods.description: Create collections that auto-expire student session data

examples.name: Student Login Session Cache (1 Hour Expiry)
examples.query: db.student_sessions.createIndex({ 'createdAt': 1 }, { expireAfterSeconds: 3600 }); db.student_sessions.insertOne({ studentId: 20260001, name: 'Daksh Chauhan', loginTime: new Date(), createdAt: new Date() })
examples.explanation: Creates session collection that auto-deletes old login sessions after 1 hour

examples.name: Student Temporary Data (24 Hour Expiry)
examples.explanation: Creates temporary collection for student uploads/processing with 24-hour auto-cleanup

examples.name: Student Activity Log (7 Day Retention)
examples.explanation: Creates activity log that keeps student actions for 7 days then auto-deletes

methods.title: Collection with Change Streams - Real-Time Student Updates
methods.description: Monitor real-time changes to student data

examples.name: Watch All Student Changes
examples.query: const changeStream = db.students.watch(); changeStream.on('change', (change) => { console.log('Student data changed:', change) })
examples.explanation: Opens change stream to monitor all student record modifications
examples.result: Real-time notification system for student updates

examples.name: Watch High GPA Students
examples.query: const changeStream = db.students.watch([ { $match: { 'operationType': 'insert', 'fullDocument.gpa': { $gte: 3.9 } } } ]); changeStream.on('change', (change) => { console.log('High GPA student added:', change.fullDocument) })
examples.explanation: Monitors only new high-performing students (GPA >= 3.9)

examples.name: Watch Student GPA Updates
examples.explanation: Monitors only GPA field updates for any student

methods.title: Copy/Filter Students to New Collections
methods.description: Create new collections by copying or filtering student data

examples.name: Copy All 60 Students to Backup
examples.explanation: Creates backup collection with all 60 student records

examples.name: High Performers (GPA >= 3.8)
examples.explanation: Creates honor roll collection with 17 students (those with GPA >= 3.8)
examples.result: Honor students collection: Anaya Sharma, Kavya Mishra, Neha Gupta, Harleen Kaur, Prisha Yadav, Yara Hassan, Maya Kapoor, Esha Chatterjee, Shreya Prabhu, Simran Kaur, Ishaan Mittal, Ishita Nair, Sudha Lakshmi, Sana Khan, Anita Malhotra, and others

examples.name: International Students Only
examples.explanation: Creates collection with only international students (30 out of 60)

examples.name: Students by Major - Data Science
examples.explanation: Creates collection with only Data Science major students
examples.result: Data Science major collection: Daksh Chauhan

examples.name: Students with Scholarship
examples.explanation: Creates collection of students with active scholarships (30 out of 60)

examples.name: Young Students (Age 20-25)
examples.explanation: Creates collection of students aged 20-25 (12 students from data.json)
examples.result: Young cohort: Daksh, Anaya, Priya, Neha, Sana, Kavya, Maya, Pooja, Ishita, Zara, Anita, Prisha

examples.name: Mature Students (Age 40+)
examples.explanation: Creates collection of older/mature students (12 students aged 40-53)

methods.title: Create Student Summary Collections via Aggregation

examples.name: Major Distribution Summary
examples.explanation: Creates summary collection with student counts, avg GPA, and scholarship totals by major

examples.name: Age Group Statistics
examples.explanation: Creates age group statistics from ages 20 to 53

examples.name: Skills Frequency Analysis
examples.explanation: Creates collection showing which skills are most common among all 60 students

examples.name: GPA Distribution Brackets
examples.query: db.gpa_brackets.insertMany(db.students.aggregate([ { $bucket: { groupBy: '$gpa', boundaries: [3.0, 3.25, 3.5, 3.75, 4.0], default: 'below3.0', output: { studentIds: { $push: '$studentId' }, count: { $sum: 1 } } } } ]).toArray())
examples.explanation: Creates GPA bracket distribution (how many students in each GPA range)

examples.name: Scholarship Statistics by Major
examples.explanation: Creates scholarship distribution by major

examples.name: Fresh Student Collection
examples.explanation: Removes old student collection and creates fresh one with clean data
examples.result: Fresh student collection with all 60 records

examples.name: Safe Drop (Even if Not Exists)
examples.explanation: Safely drops collection if exists, then creates new empty one

methods.title: Collection with Complex Student Schema Validation
methods.description: Advanced validation matching exact data.json structure

examples.name: Complete Student Schema with All Validations
examples.query: db.createCollection('students', { validator: { $jsonSchema: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['studentId', 'name', 'age', 'enrollmentDate', 'major', 'gpa', 'isInternational', 'skills', 'courses', 'campusLocation', 'scholarshipDetails', 'libraryCardToken'], properties: { studentId: { bsonType: 'long', description: 'Unique student ID like 20260001' }, name: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['first', 'last'], properties: { first: { bsonType: 'string', minLength: 1, maxLength: 100, description: 'First name (Daksh, Anaya, etc)' }, last: { bsonType: 'string', minLength: 1, maxLength: 100, description: 'Last name (Chauhan, Sharma, etc)' } } }, age: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 18, maximum: 65, description: 'Student age (20-53 in current data)' }, enrollmentDate: { bsonType: 'date', description: 'Date student enrolled' }, major: { enum: ['Data Science', 'Frontend Engineering', 'Machine Learning', 'Cloud Computing', 'Cybersecurity', 'Mobile Development', 'Backend Engineering', 'Artificial Intelligence', 'DevOps Engineering', 'Game Development', 'Full Stack Development', 'Blockchain Development', 'Software Architecture', 'QA Automation', 'Data Engineering', 'UI/UX Design', 'IoT Solutions', 'Technical Writing', 'Database Administration', 'Solution Architecture', 'Network Engineering', 'Product Management', 'Computer Vision', 'Cyber Threat Intelligence', 'Robotics Engineering', 'API Development', 'Site Reliability Engineering', 'Augmented Reality', 'Quantum Computing', 'Bioinformatics', 'Enterprise Software', 'Embedded Systems', 'Web Services Architecture', 'Accessibility Engineering', 'Performance Engineering', 'Natural Language Processing', 'Fintech Development', 'Environmental Computing', 'Healthcare IT', 'E-Commerce Development', 'Marketing Technology', 'Supply Chain Tech', 'Content Delivery Systems', 'Information Security', 'Voice Technology', 'Streaming Platforms', 'Software Testing', 'Container Orchestration', 'Graph Database Design', 'Search Engine Architecture', 'Analytics Engineering', 'Compliance Engineering', 'Edge Computing', 'Software Security', 'API Governance', 'Cloud Security', 'Low-Code Platforms', 'Incident Management', 'Data Privacy', 'Observability Engineering'], description: '60 different majors in current dataset' }, gpa: { bsonType: 'decimal', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 4.0, description: 'GPA between 3.21 and 3.98 in current data' }, isInternational: { bsonType: 'bool', description: 'Whether student is international (true/false)' }, skills: { bsonType: 'array', minItems: 3, maxItems: 10, items: { bsonType: 'string' }, description: 'List of technical skills like Python, Java, React, etc' }, courses: { bsonType: 'array', minItems: 1, items: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['courseCode', 'credits', 'grade'], properties: { courseCode: { bsonType: 'string', pattern: '^[A-Z]{1,20}-[0-9]{3}$' }, credits: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 1, maximum: 4 }, grade: { enum: ['A+', 'A', 'A-', 'B+', 'B', 'B-', 'C+', 'C', 'C-', 'D', 'F'] } } } }, campusLocation: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['type', 'coordinates'], properties: { type: { enum: ['Point'] }, coordinates: { bsonType: 'array', minItems: 2, maxItems: 2, items: { bsonType: 'double' }, description: 'Longitude and latitude coordinates' } } }, scholarshipDetails: { oneOf: [{ bsonType: 'null' }, { bsonType: 'object', required: ['scholarshipName', 'amount', 'renewable'], properties: { scholarshipName: { bsonType: 'string' }, amount: { bsonType: 'int', minimum: 5000, maximum: 50000 }, renewable: { bsonType: 'bool' } } }] }, libraryCardToken: { bsonType: 'object', required: ['$binary'], properties: { '$binary': { bsonType: 'object', properties: { base64: { bsonType: 'string' }, subType: { enum: ['00'] } } } } } }, additionalProperties: false } } })
examples.explanation: Validates all 60 student records matching exact data.json structure

examples.name: Create All Student Collections at Once
examples.explanation: Creates 5 student-related collections in one operation

examples.name: Create Collections with Specific Purposes
examples.query: db.createCollection('active_students'); db.createCollection('graduated_students', { capped: true, size: 10485760 }); db.createCollection('student_archive', { size: 52428800 }); db.createCollection('student_temp_data', { expireAfterSeconds: 3600 })
examples.explanation: Creates multiple student collections for different purposes

methods.title: Create Student Collections with Campus Location Optimization
methods.description: Optimize for geospatial queries on campus locations

examples.name: Students by Campus Location
examples.explanation: Creates geospatial index and finds students within 5km of NYC campus (40.7306, -73.9352)

examples.name: Students in Geographic Region
examples.explanation: Finds all students within a geographic bounding box

methods.title: Complete Production-Grade Student Collection Setup
methods.description: Enterprise-ready student collection with all features

examples.name: Production Student System Collection
examples.query: db.createCollection('students', { capped: false, size: 209715200, max: 100000, validator: { $jsonSchema: { [Full schema from method 12] } }, validationLevel: 'strict', validationAction: 'error', collation: { locale: 'en_US', strength: 2 }, comment: 'Primary production collection for 60 students with comprehensive validation' }); db.students.createIndex({ 'studentId': 1 }, { unique: true }); db.students.createIndex({ 'major': 1 }); db.students.createIndex({ 'gpa': -1 }); db.students.createIndex({ 'age': 1 }); db.students.createIndex({ 'campusLocation': '2dsphere' }); db.students.createIndex({ 'skills': 'text' }); db.students.createIndex({ 'isInternational': 1 })
examples.explanation: Creates fully optimized production student collection with all validations, collation, and indexes
examples.result: Enterprise-grade student system ready for 60+ student records
